automated_tests: Confirm formulas.txt contains entries for budget aggregation and per-option post-remediation cells
Confirm budget_exceeded cell is present and evaluates correctly when formulas.txt is parsed
Confirm per-record post-remediation table matches published key_results

automated_tests: Confirm formulas.txt contains entries for budget aggregation and per-option post-remediation cells
Confirm budget_exceeded cell is present and evaluates correctly when formulas.txt is parsed
Confirm per-record post-remediation table matches published key_results
extra_model_fields.notes: This revision is scoped to improve reproducibility and automated verifiability (local, low-complexity changes).

planned_changes: Add a 'Formulas' worksheet that contains the exact formulas used in key calculation cells (budget aggregation, per-option post-remediation metrics, classification logic). Ensure formulas are visible (no cell protection).
Export the formulas worksheet as a plain-text file (formulas.txt) listing cell addresses and formula expressions and attach it to the deliverables.
Add a visible 'budget_exceeded' boolean cell (e.g., cell with =SUM(option_costs) and =SUM(...)<=budget) and include the explicit SUM formula in the Formulas sheet.

In the Packet or an Analysis sheet, add per-record post-remediation numeric columns for each option (showing the metric(s) after O1/O2/O3) plus the resulting classification (overcall/undercall/neither) computed with the exact threshold formulas.

Update the expert memo: (a) add a one-line explicit decision objective used for ranking options, (b) resolve or explicitly comment on each 'needs_review' assumption, and (c) add a short pointer to the Formulas sheet and the exported formulas text file.

Unprotect/unhide any sheets needed for verification or provide a short Verification sheet that lists representative cell addresses and their intended values so graders can quickly inspect or parse them.
Re-run the analysis, update key_results in actual_outputs, and attach the revised spreadsheet plus the exported-formulas file and the updated memo.

Before finalizing, run a minimal automated parse check in the current environment (or CI) to confirm that the formulas.txt and per-record tables are sufficient for programmatic verification even if openpyxl is unavailable.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve machine-verifiability and remove grader uncertainty caused by inability to parse spreadsheet formulas; make decision objective explicit and expose per-record post-remediation values.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables are present and the expert memo documents methods, thresholds, per-record calculations, sensitivity analysis, and a clear recommendation (O2). Grader and verifier awarded full artifact-contract credit but noted a minor deduction because the environment could not programmatically parse the spreadsheet (openpyxl unavailable). This produced residual uncertainty about spreadsheet formulas and budget-aggregation formulas. The memo is high quality; the workbook should be made explicitly machine-verifiable.
